name: Apricot Souffles
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup sugar plus additional for coating
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, simmer apricots, water, and 1/2 cup sugar covered for 20 minutes.
2. Transfer hot mixture to a food processor and puree until very smooth.
3. Force the puree through a fine sieve into a bowl and stir in lemon juice, rum, vanilla, and a pinch of salt. Let it cool completely. The puree can be made up to 2 days ahead and chilled, covered. Bring the puree to room temperature before proceeding.
4. Transfer the puree to a large bowl. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Generously butter six 7-ounce (3 1/2 by 1 3/4-inch) ramekins and coat with additional sugar, removing excess.
6. In another large bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until foamy. Beat in cream of tartar and continue beating until the egg whites hold soft peaks.
7. Gradually beat in the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, a little at a time, until the meringue holds stiff peaks. Whisk about one fourth of the meringue into the puree to lighten it, then gently fold in the remaining meringue thoroughly.
8. Ladle batter into ramekins and bake souffles on a baking sheet in the middle of the oven for 20 to 25 minutes, or until puffed, golden brown, and just set in the center.
9. Remove ramekins from the oven. With two forks, gently open the center of each souffle and pour some crème anglaise into each opening.
10. Serve immediately.
Yield: 6 servings
